title: erythranthe washingtonensis
intro: Erythranthe washingtonensis, also known as the Olympic monkey flower or Washington monkey flower, is a species of flowering plant in the family Phrymaceae. It is native to the Pacific Northwest region of North America, specifically in the Olympic Peninsula in Washington state. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory effects, making it useful for treating wounds and skin conditions. Erythranthe washingtonensis can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, which have been used to support respiratory health and alleviate symptoms of coughs and colds.
